name: Media Capture Studio
description: Camera, screen recording, and audio recording straight from the browser — capture, encoding, and a custom player, no plugins and no upload.
longDescription: Three media capture demos that share the same technical foundation: camera and microphone access via getUserMedia, screen sharing via getDisplayMedia, and recording of every stream with the MediaRecorder API — including automatic codec negotiation (VP9 → VP8 → plain WebM) through MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported. Each recording becomes a local Blob, played back in an audio/video player built from scratch instead of relying on the browser's native controls: play/pause, a requestAnimationFrame-driven progress bar, and click-to-seek. Everything runs in the browser — no media byte ever leaves the user's device.
tags: getUserMedia, getDisplayMedia, MediaRecorder, Camera API, Screen Recording, Audio Recording, Blob, Codec Negotiation
architecture: MediaStream capture via browser APIs (getUserMedia/getDisplayMedia) with client-side encoding through MediaRecorder, including codec negotiation and a custom player rendered from the resulting Blob
technologies: MediaDevices API, MediaRecorder API, Blob, URL.createObjectURL, requestAnimationFrame
concepts: Browser media permissions, codec negotiation (MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported), real-time stream capture, client-side encoding, Blob and Object URL lifecycle, custom media player, local download with no server upload
cameraDesc1: The Camera API is used to access the device's camera directly from the browser.
cameraDesc2: In practice, it lets web applications display the camera's live video feed inside the app.
cameraCanDoItem6: capture an image for authentication or documents.
cameraVsRecorderDesc: Despite the similar name, it isn't quite the same thing as the MediaRecorder API.
cameraGetUserMediaDesc: The Camera API is normally responsible for accessing the live camera feed through getUserMedia().
cameraRecorderRoleDesc: The MediaRecorder API, on the other hand, is used to record that audio/video stream into files.
cameraSummaryItem1: Camera API: accesses and displays the camera in real time.
cameraSummaryItem2: MediaRecorder API: records audio and video captured from the camera and microphone.
cameraCombinedNote: In this demo, the two technologies work together: the Camera API delivers the live stream, and the MediaRecorder API records that same stream for playback and download — the same pattern used in video calls with recording, selfie-based authentication, and identity-verification apps.
cameraWarningText: Heads up: to use this feature, you need to allow access to your device's camera. The video is processed entirely in the browser: nothing is sent, stored, or collected on a server.
cameraHowToUseText: Click "Capture camera" to start recording. Click it again (now "Stop capturing camera") to finish. The recorded video shows up in the player below, ready for playback and download.
cameraUnsupportedMessage: Camera capture isn't supported in this browser. Use an up-to-date browser to access this feature.
cameraMethod1: navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() — requests access to the device camera and returns a MediaStream
cameraMethod2: new MediaRecorder(stream) — creates a recorder instance from a MediaStream
cameraMethod3: MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported(mimeType) — checks whether the video format is supported by the browser
cameraMethod4: MediaRecorder.start() — starts capture and begins accumulating video data
cameraMethod5: MediaRecorder.stop() — ends capture and fires the onstop event
cameraMethod6: MediaRecorder.ondataavailable — event fired periodically with the recorded data chunks
cameraMethod7: MediaRecorder.onstop — event fired when recording ends, used to assemble the final Blob
cameraMethod8: new Blob(chunks, '{ type }') — combines the accumulated chunks into a single video file
cameraMethod9: URL.createObjectURL(blob) — generates a temporary URL to play back or download the recorded video
cameraMethod10: stream.getTracks()[0].onended — detects when the user stops the capture from the browser itself
screenDesc1: The MediaRecorder API can also record video directly from the browser — in this case, capturing the device's screen instead of the camera.
screenDesc2: In practice, it lets web applications capture video in real time without installing any external software.
screenCanDoItem1: record video from the device's screen;
screenCanDoItem5: create experiences that feel close to native apps.
screenUseCasesDesc: This combination of APIs is the technical foundation behind meeting platforms, lecture recording, content creation, and video-based verification systems.
screenPwaNote: In PWAs, the MediaRecorder API helps turn web applications into far more interactive, multimedia-rich experiences.
screenPermissionNote: Just like camera and microphone access, the browser always asks for explicit user permission before allowing screen sharing.
screenWarningText: Heads up: to use this feature, you need to allow screen sharing for your device. The video is processed entirely in the browser: nothing is sent, stored, or collected on a server.
screenHowToUseText: Click "Share screen" to start recording and choose what to share (entire screen, window, or tab) in the browser's dialog. Click it again (now "Stop sharing screen") to finish. The recorded video shows up in the player below, ready for playback and download.
screenUnsupportedMessage: Screen sharing isn't supported in this browser. Use an up-to-date desktop browser to access this feature.
screenMethod1: navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia() — requests access to the device screen and returns a MediaStream
screenMethod2: new MediaRecorder(stream) — creates a recorder instance from a MediaStream
screenMethod3: MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported(mimeType) — checks whether the video format is supported by the browser
screenMethod4: MediaRecorder.start() — starts capture and begins accumulating video data
screenMethod5: MediaRecorder.stop() — ends capture and fires the onstop event
screenMethod6: MediaRecorder.ondataavailable — event fired periodically with the recorded data chunks
screenMethod7: MediaRecorder.onstop — event fired when recording ends, used to assemble the final Blob
screenMethod8: new Blob(chunks, '{ type }') — combines the accumulated chunks into a single video file
screenMethod9: URL.createObjectURL(blob) — generates a temporary URL to play back or download the recorded video
screenMethod10: stream.getTracks()[0].onended — detects when the user stops sharing from the browser itself
audioDesc1: The MediaRecorder API is the same browser API used to record video — here applied to capturing audio from the device's microphone.
audioDesc2: Combined with the microphone, it lets you create audio recordings directly in the browser, with no external software installed.
audioCanDoItem5: create experiences similar to native apps.
audioMessagingExample: A very common example is messaging apps that let you send voice messages straight from the browser.
audioOtherExamples: Another example is meeting platforms, podcast recording, online classes, and voice-based support systems.
audioPermissionNote: For security and privacy reasons, the browser always asks for explicit user permission before granting access to the microphone or camera.
audioWarningText: Heads up: to use this feature, you need to allow access to your device's microphone. The audio is processed entirely in the browser: nothing is sent, stored, or collected on a server.
audioHowToUseText: Click "Record" to start. Click it again (now "Recording") to finish. The recorded audio shows up in the player below in Opus/OGG format, ready for playback and download.
audioUnsupportedMessage: Audio recording isn't supported in this browser. Use an up-to-date browser to access this feature.
audioGenericError: Error accessing the microphone. Check whether permission was granted.
audioMethod1: new MediaRecorder(stream).start() — starts capturing audio from the device
audioMethod2: new MediaRecorder(stream).stop() — ends capture and fires the onstop event, which assembles the final Blob as audio/ogg; codecs=opus
